subject: English
title: The Adventures of Toto – Hard Short Notes

key_points: The story narrates the humorous yet troublesome experiences of keeping a monkey as a pet
Toto’s intelligence and curiosity often lead to chaos and destruction
Several episodes such as the railway journey, bath scene and lunch incident highlight his unpredictable behaviour
Grandfather admires Toto’s cleverness but the family cannot manage the damage he causes
The ending emphasises the reality that wild animals cannot easily adapt to domestic life
characters: Toto – Symbol of curiosity, mischief and uncontrolled energy
Grandfather – Represents affection for animals and tolerance
Grandmother – Symbolises practicality and household discipline
Narrator – Observer who recounts the humorous experiences
themes: The clash between human order and animal instinct
The limits of human control over wild nature
literary_devices: Irony – Funny misunderstandings like the ticket collector calling Toto a dog
Humour – Used throughout the narrative to entertain readers
Symbolism – Toto symbolises curiosity and untamed nature
exam_tips: Analyse Toto’s character using examples such as the kettle and lunch incidents
Discuss humour and irony as major narrative techniques
Relate Toto’s behaviour to the central theme of wild instincts
Explain how the ending reinforces the story’s message
